step_1_counts.note: Step 1 metadata.total_papers was 48 but papers[] list had 49; corrected here.

canonical_checklist.source_policy: Written in Phase 2 BEFORE examining Step 1 output — per §7 Phase 2 and §13 Anti-Pattern 1. See _phase2_checklist_WIP.json for original unredacted form.
pathways: Core auxin perception (TIR1/AFB1-5; Aux/IAAs IAA14/SLR, IAA28, IAA12/BDL, IAA3/SHY2, IAA19; ARFs ARF7/19, ARF5/MP, ARF6/8)
LR founder cell specification (GATA23, IAA28, pericycle prebranch oscillator, WRKY23, auxin maxima)
LR initiation asymmetric cell division (IAA14 -> ARF7+ARF19 -> LBD16/18/29 -> cyclins; PUCHI; TOLS2-RLK7)
LR primordium organogenesis (PLT3/5/7; WOX11/12 founder-to-organ; WOX5/7; SHR/SCR radial)
LR emergence (LAX3 influx in endodermis, cell-wall remodelling, ACC signalling)
Auxin biosynthesis (TAA1, TAR1, TAR2; YUC1-9 inc YUC4/6; WEI2/ASA1 + WEI7/ASB1 Trp biosynthesis)
Auxin polar transport (PIN1/2/3/4/7/8, AUX1, LAX3, ABCB19, GNOM, PID/WAG kinases, CIPK-CBL)
Cytokinin module (IPT3/5/7, CKX1-7, AHK2/3/4 receptors, AHP1-5, type-B ARR1/10/12, type-A ARR3-16)
Nitrate signalling (NRT1.1/CHL1 dual transceptor, ANR1, AFB3->NAC4->OBP4, CLE1/3/4/7->CLV1 feedback, TAR2, CIPK8/23)
Phosphate signalling (LPR1/2, STOP1, ALMT1, PDR2, PHR1/PHL1, SIZ1, PHT transporters)
Ethylene (ACS, ACO, ETR1/2, ERS1/2, EIN4, CTR1, EIN2, EIN3/EIL1, WEI2/WEI7 induction)
Mechanical/gravity/hydrotropism (MIZ1, PIN3 relocalisation, bending auxin asymmetry)
canonical_mutants: tir1 afb1 afb2 afb3 afb4 afb5 quintuple
environmental_inputs: Nitrate (homogeneous high vs patchy vs low <100 uM)
crosstalk: Auxin <-> cytokinin via SHY2/IAA3 -> PIN repression (Bielach 2012)
Nitrate <-> auxin via NRT1.1 (auxin efflux) and ANR1 -> TAR2 biosynthesis (Ma 2014)
Ethylene <-> auxin via TAA1/TAR2 + WEI2/WEI7 induction (Stepanova 2005, 2007, 2008)
ABA <-> nitrate via ABI4/ABI5 repressing nitrate-induced LR (Shkolnik-Inbar 2010)
GA <-> LBD via DELLA interaction with SCR-like TFs
Mechanical bending -> PIN3 relocalisation -> local auxin maxima
BR -> BIN2/BZR1/BES1 crosstalk with N availability for LR (Al-Mamun 2024)

gaps.biological_justification: TAA1, TAR2 and YUCCAs drive LR-specific IAA production. Step 1 captured enzyme->Auxin edges but missed upstream regulators — notably ethylene transcriptionally induces TAA1/TAR family (Stepanova 2008 Cell) and induces WEI2/WEI7 anthranilate synthase subunits (Stepanova 2005). No WEI2/WEI7 nodes were in Step 1.
search_queries: Stepanova 2007 ethylene TAA1 WEI2 WEI7 auxin biosynthesis root
Stepanova 2008 TAA1 TAR2 auxin biosynthesis hormone crosstalk

gaps.biological_justification: Step 1 had a generic CLE->LR edge and a CLV1->CLE2 edge but did NOT include the Araya 2014 primary paper, nor CLE3/CLE1/CLE4/CLE7 as N-induced pericycle peptides repressing LR via phloem CLV1. This is the canonical systemic-N feedback module of the field and must be present so BUILDER can model the shoot-demand feedback loop.
search_queries: CLE3 CLE1 CLV1 nitrogen systemic lateral root Araya 2014
Araya 2014 CLE CLV1 lateral root PNAS

gaps.biological_justification: The two-step cell-fate transition (WOX11/12 -> LBD16/29 for founder; WOX11/12 -> WOX5/7 for primordium stem cell niche) is textbook for root primordium development and provides a parallel LBD16/29 activation route alongside ARF7/ARF19. Step 1 had ZERO WOX11 or WOX12 edges.
search_queries: WOX11 WOX12 lateral root founder cell transition Arabidopsis primordium

gaps.biological_justification: Without this arm, ethylene only directly inhibits LR (Negi 2008 edges) and enhances AUX1 transport. But the ethylene-Trp-Auxin loop (Stepanova 2005, 2007, 2008) is a mechanism whereby ethylene drives local auxin spikes — critical for correct ethylene-auxin interactions in LR modelling.
search_queries: Stepanova 2008 TAA1 TAR2 ethylene auxin biosynthesis
WEI2 WEI7 ASA1 ASB1 ethylene root

gaps.pathway: PID / WAG AGC3-kinase PIN polarity control
gaps.biological_justification: Step 1 had PIN1/2/3/7 biology but no PIN phosphorylation regulators. PID and WAG1/WAG2 kinases phosphorylate PIN TPRXS motifs to switch PIN polarity between basal and apical — directly relevant to gravity-induced PIN3 relocation and LR initiation asymmetry (Grones 2018). Without these nodes, modelling of PIN-switch biology is impossible.
search_queries: PID WAG kinase PIN polarity phosphorylation lateral root Arabidopsis

gaps.pathway: Brassinosteroid BIN2 -> BZR1/BES1 control of LR
gaps.biological_justification: Step 1 had BRI1->BZR1 (edge E203) but no BIN2, no BZR1->LR, no BES1->LR, and no mechanistic dose-dependent BR effects. Al-Mamun 2024 quantitatively documents bzr1-1D and bes1-D LR enhancement under varied N.
search_queries: BIN2 BZR1 BES1 lateral root Arabidopsis brassinosteroid bri1

gaps.biological_justification: Step 1 had only ARR1 (type-B) and ARR15 (type-A) among response regulators. ARR10 and ARR12 are the other two major redundant type-B regulators in roots. The arr1,10,12 triple is the canonical CK-insensitive mutant; absence of ARR10/ARR12 nodes prevents BUILDER from representing type-B redundancy.
search_queries: ARR10 ARR12 ARR1 type-B lateral root Arabidopsis cytokinin

gaps.biological_justification: ABI5 is a canonical ABA bZIP TF that negatively regulates LR under stress and is required (with ABI4) for nitrate-induced LR inhibition. Step 1 had ABA, ABI4, SnRK2 but zero ABI5 edges.
search_queries: ABI5 lateral root Arabidopsis ABA abi5 mutant

missing_mutants: tir1 afb1 afb2 afb3 afb4 afb5 quintuple
gaps.biological_justification: Standard multi-mutants + Low_Nitrate environmental absent from perturbation_dataset.json. Step 1 had high singleton-mutant coverage but missed canonical higher-order combinations used in textbook LR genetics.

Low_Phosphate is an ENVIRONMENT node. By convention environmental nodes are source-only (no upstream biology in the modelled network), so this is structurally expected. Kept on the gap list as a LOW-severity noted item rather than attempted closure.
gaps.residual_note: BUILDER should declare Low_Phosphate and Low_Nitrate as is_source=true in network.json (see Trap 5 in CLAUDE.md).

bidirectional_audit.comment: Low_Nitrate became source-only post-merge because Step 1.5 added outgoing environmental edges (Low_Nitrate->CLE3, Low_Nitrate->CLE1, Low_Nitrate->LR) without upstream regulation. This is expected — ENVIRONMENT nodes are by design source-only. Step 1 had Low_Nitrate->TAR2 only so it was already effectively source-only with out=1 (below the >=3 flag threshold). No GENE nodes are source-only in either state.
bidirectional_audit.resolution_for_genes: All gene hubs now have >=1 incoming edge post-merge.

extraction_density_audit.zero_edge_papers_note: The 2 read papers (candidate_papers.json status='read') but with zero edges in curated_edges.json likely reflect papers used only as conceptual framing (Peret 2009 Trends Plant Sci review; Marhavy 2016 G&D). Not re-read in Step 1.5 since they are review/overview and the biology they summarise is independently curated via other papers.
extraction_density_audit.comment: Step 1 extraction density was strong: means of 35-39 edges from the two Cavallari 2021 / Roychoudhry 2022 reviews and 12-24 edges for topic-specific OA papers. No shallow-read papers needed re-extraction. Gap-driven searches added 10 new papers with 40 new edges (mean 4.0 edges/paper for Step 1.5).

residual_gaps_for_builder: Source-node policy: BUILDER must set is_source=true for Low_Nitrate and Low_Phosphate (ENVIRONMENT, expected source nodes). Currently both have out-edges but no in-edges post-merge; this is correct biology for environmental inputs.

BAM1 receptor node (added via G002) is a redundant partner of CLV1 with only 2 edges in the merged repository (CLE3->BAM1, BAM1->LR). BUILDER may either (a) include BAM1 alongside CLV1 for redundancy representation, or (b) collapse BAM1 into CLV1 as a composite receptor. Document choice.

WAG1/WAG2 each have only 1 edge (WAG1->PIN3, WAG2->PIN3). They are redundant with PID but narrow in curation scope. BUILDER may collapse PID/WAG1/WAG2 into a single kinase node or include only PID with a literature_gap annotation noting WAG redundancy.

YUC6 does not appear in curated_edges at all (YUC4 is present) despite YUC6 being canonical. Unclosable in this round — no OA paper found with specific YUC6 LR mechanism + direct-binding evidence. BUILDER should represent YUC-family via YUC4 only or add literature_gap for YUC6.
IPT3 and IPT7 cytokinin biosynthesis genes are mentioned indirectly (Auxin->IPT5) but IPT3 and IPT7 themselves have no edges. BUILDER may retain the IPT family as IPT5 alone or flag literature_gap.
PIF-independent thermomorphogenesis is irrelevant for LR (PIF4 is shoot-focused), correctly absent.
E211+ edges carry provenance 'discovery_source: literature_judge' and 'gap_addressed: G0XX' fields. BUILDER may filter or stratify by these if helpful but they are not required for network selection.

merge_provenance.id_invariants: No Step-1 IDs were renumbered or modified. Merges were strictly additive.
